meta.description: Semantic equivalences between layer1 (BFO+GeoCore) and layer1b (GeoSciML+CGI Simple Lithology)

mappings.rationale: Both represent a volume of rock with distinct lithological character and defined extent; GeoCore GeologicUnit and GeoSciML GeologicUnit share the same conceptual scope and are used interchangeably in cross-standard alignment

GeoCore GeologicBody (a body of rock with defined extent and lithological character) is the functional equivalent of GeoSciML GeologicUnit; both are used in the Geolytics entity-graph for bacia-sedimentar, reservatorio, and formacao nodes

Both represent a drilled hole in the earth; GeoSciML gsmlbh:Borehole extends the GeoCore concept with detailed observation intervals, drilling method, and origin position — fully interchangeable at the class level
Both are the abstract superclass for all geological structures (faults, folds, foliations, lineations, contacts); direct conceptual equivalence

GeoCore FaultZone (a zone of sheared rock along a fault plane) is narrower than GeoSciML ShearDisplacementStructure, which also encompasses ductile shear zones, thrust sheets, and detachments beyond brittle fault zones
Both represent the surface or narrow zone separating two geologic units; identical conceptual scope across GeoCore and GeoSciML
Both represent a curvilinear structure produced by ductile deformation of rock layers; identical conceptual scope
Both represent a planar penetrative fabric in rock (schistosity, cleavage, banding); identical conceptual scope
Both represent a linear penetrative fabric in rock (stretching lineation, mineral lineation, crenulation lineation); identical conceptual scope
Both represent the material composition of a rock body; direct counterparts in the two ontologies
CGI sedimentary_material is a specific subtype of GeoCore RockMaterial; the CGI vocabulary provides the canonical enumeration of lithology classes that GeoCore defines abstractly

GeoCore GeologicFeature is the abstract geological concept; GeoSciML MappedFeature is specifically the cartographic representation of that concept at a given map scale — broader in GeoCore, narrower and more concrete in GeoSciML
Both represent a discrete event in geological time (e.g. a phase of deformation, intrusion, or deposition); identical conceptual scope
Both are the top-level class for any naturally occurring solid material forming the Earth; direct equivalents
